title: centaurea jacea subsp. amara
intro: Centaurea jacea subsp. amara, commonly known as bitter knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Centaurea jacea subsp. amara can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
